raw_text: liam ritchie theaker 18 blenheim gardens, kingston upon thames, kt2 7bw, united kingdom tel: +447709 141 694 email: liamrt@hotmail.co.uk an experienced professional who has delivered comprehensive aircraft maintenance, within multiple organisations across europe. extensive multicultural experience working in a technical capacity, is comfortable within complex, international environments. passionate learner, demonstrated by a return to education. gaining further understanding of engineering principles, performing group and individual projects. education and qualifications kingston university, london bsc (hons) aircraft engineering, september 2016 may 2017 course covers a broad range of subjects including aerospace technology, air transport economics, aircraft maintenance operations and a final, individual project. civil aviation authority easa b1.1 aircraft maintenance licence, september 2013 june 2016 completed numerous theoretical modules and satisfied practical experience criteria to qualify as an aircraft maintenance engineer. academically equivalent to 240 credits at level 4 and 5. lufthansa resource technical training easa category a licence, october 2004 january 2007 followed a comprehensive training programme ultimately qualifying as an aircraft technician. north nottinghamshire college, october 2004 january 2007 achieved nvq level 3 in aircraft engineering maintenance and btec level 3 in engineering. vermuyden school gcses, september 1999 may 2004 9 subjects in total including english, mathematics and science. work experience cargolux airlines, luxembourg aircraft maintenance technician, november 2013 august 2016 e employed full-time to perform line and hangar maintenance on boeing 747-8 and 747-400 aircraft during main base / daily / weekly / a and c checks. e successfully completed mandatory courses, such as aircraft type course. e tasks included defect troubleshooting, fault diagnosis, inspection, component removal / installation / servicing, operational testing and creation of non-routine paperwork. e liaised with necessary departments in order to fulfil required tasks and duties. aircraft technician (contractor), january 2007 october 2013 (full-time employment secured through several high-quality aviation recruitment agencies) e provided wide-ranging services to clients across europe. these included cargolux (luxembourg), lufthansa technik (switzerland), vt aerospace (uk), flybe (uk), jet aviation (switzerland) and swiss int. airlines (switzerland). e managed the end to end life cycle of maintenance removal, inspection, rectification, installation projects working across multiple aircraft types. this demonstrated an ability to apply core engineering expertise within a technical environment. e asacontractor had to adapt quickly to new companies and countries. e closely monitored all paperwork to ensure both company and industry protocols were adhered to. e managed complex problem solving activities and had autonomous decision making powers to implement resolutions. e mentored junior staff, introducing them to procedures and providing support, demonstrating leaderships skills. e applications used included trax, amos and ms office. e aircraft types: airbus a320 family, avro rj 85/100, boeing 727, 737, 747, 767, bombardier challenger, q300/q400, embraer 135/145, falcon 50, 2000, gulfstream g4, saab 2000, tucano t1. lufthansa resource technical training apprentice, october 2004 january 2007 gained knowledge and experience and qualified as an aircraft maintenance technician. skills organisational constantly demonstrates excellent organisational skills through the completion of numerous, simultaneous tasks and projects, before the required deadline. has demonstrated this in both industrial and academic environments. communicational provides high quality written and verbal communication, proving to be clear and concise when delivering information, and efficient when receiving information. problem resolution has faced problems in technical and non-technical environments. effectively uses the necessary resources to find the most suitable solutions. additional information language proficiency: english: mother tongue + spanish: level b, independent user e french: level a, basic user interests and activities: travel spends a lot of leisure time visiting new places. on a longer trip in 2010, visited central america and volunteered within a clean-up project after a major storm and in an organisation for under-privileged children. plays a lot of sports including squash and competitive football. has demonstrated excellent teamworking skills within numerous teams in various locations. references: available on request.
